name: Q188969
description: American blues guitarist, singer, and songwriter (1925–2015)
abstract: Riley B. King (September 16, 1925 – May 14, 2015), known professionally as B.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Present Company Included

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: And I took off and went on a road with B.B. King in the hopes of just setting up a financial base to really set myself up in New York, which turned out to be a three-year journey around the world.

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I made some recordings with B. B. King. He would call me in to, I wasn‘t with his group or anything, but he would call me in to record with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Baron
about_quotes.evidence: I’m very lucky, I played with Stevie Wonder, Duke Ellington, B.B. King, other black groups you know?
about_quotes.doc_title: Arthur Baron interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 3, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: We played on the same ticket with B.B. King at the Blue Eagle.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: We was all hangin' out down there together... B.B. King an' all them fellows, they were doin' the same thing... We used to all meet up together in this restaurant and have a ball.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: in my neighborhood B.B. King, Howlin’ Wolf, Muddy Waters, Bo Diddley, you name it, there was a cafe in my neighborhood under the hill that these guys used to come weekly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024
